publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-05-28 23:46:46 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection includes manuscripts and typescripts of three novels THE SHEEPFOLD, THE OUTPOSTS OF COLD, and BRIEF CANDLE, short stories, poetry, reviews, critiques, and essays; polemics; literary studies; notes; student work; broadsides; correspondence and other records concerning "Epoch" and "Cornell Review"; correspondence (approximately one quarter of the collection) to and from Hathaway, particularly with David Ray, B.H. Friedman, and editors and publishers;

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Baxter Levering Hathaway (1909-1984) was editor of the CORNELL REVIEW.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes manuscripts and typescripts of three novels THE SHEEPFOLD, THE OUTPOSTS OF COLD, and BRIEF CANDLE, short stories, poetry, reviews, critiques, and essays; polemics; literary studies; notes; student work; broadsides; correspondence and other records concerning "Epoch" and "Cornell Review"; correspondence (approximately one quarter of the collection) to and from Hathaway, particularly with David Ray, B.H. Friedman, and editors and publishers;

material concerning the 1938 Michigan Hopwood Award, including submissions by Hathaway and "A.J. Joachim"; curriculum studies; drafts of textbooks; and publications and newsletters concerning national and international writing programs and humanities programs.

The collection is well described by Hathaway's "I am interested in grammar, rhetoric, and poetics, intellectual and cultural history. I have spent much time on the development of poets and novelists, on modern literary aesthetics, on the editing of a literary magazine, on my own variety of transformational grammar, and on the literary criticism of the Renaissance and Neo-classical period.
"I am also interested in work/study collaborations at Ithaca House, in publishing techniques and practices and the small press revolution."

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Baxter Hathaway papers, #14-12-2198. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Written communication.
Rhetoric.
Renaissance.
Publishers.
Polemics.
Poetry.
Philology.
Neo-classicism (Literary)
Little presses.
Literature -- Study and teaching.
Literature -- History and criticism.
Literature -- Competitions.
Literature, Modern.
Literary prizes.
Humanities.
Generative grammar.
Essays.
Educators.
Critics.
Criticism.
Authors.
American literature -- Periodicals.
Aesthetics.
controlaccess.title: Epoch.
Cornell review.
controlaccess.persname: Hathaway, Baxter, 1909- (Title of work: Outposts of cold..)

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid. PDF file.

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of English: Freshman English Program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials concerning the development of a first-year composition course. Includes faculty letters and perspectives, Writing Workshop Report, and newspaper clippings.

did.unittitle: Advisory Council of the College of Arts and Sciences

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minute notes, schedules, and agendas for the council. Included is a survey from the Commission on the Humanities with Hathaway's handwritten response.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and other items pertaining to the literary journal; newspaper clippings and announcement of premier issue; letters; memos, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "History." Includes newspaper clippings, letters, and start-up material for the review.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General information; Salina Press; Cornell Alumni News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publication cost information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subscription income

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personnel information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists of payments to authors for contribution of work to the journal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Information sent to the Editorial Board. Includes meeting minutes, memos, and list of material for future issues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Left-over photographs from journal. Also graphs and charts of scientific interest on negative paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of purpose in establishing the review; letters of solicitation to "Friends of Cornell"; individuals' requests for sample copies of the review; lists of people receiving complimentary copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complimentary copies to subscribers, authors, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Patrons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reactions to first issue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills, invoices, and customer copies for the review.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Employee and payroll information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright assignments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composer bids.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Distribution information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printing division charges.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small order forms--Cornell University invoices

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Financial records of the review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typewriter repair shop invoices.

scopecontent.p: Bookstore purchases

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional Service record

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Essays and articles about sociobiology, Darwinism, and evolution. Information on Philosophy 246 (Environmental Ethics) and essay "On Teaching Environmental Ethics." Also information on Philosophy 286 (Science and Human Nature); notes; pamphlet "Cornell University Program on Science, Technology, and Society."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters and questions to specialists in the field of anthropology in an attempt to answer the question, "Are All Cultures Equal?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Hazard Adams (Dept. of English and Comparative Lit. University of California, Irvine), Paul Anbinder (President, Nelson Rockefeller Publications, Inc.), and Rob Allen (Champlain Regional College)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Cynthia Buchanan, Kenneth E. Boulding, and Fred Buell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Cheever, Tony Caputi, and Frederick Cassidy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Peter T. Daniels ('72), Otto C. Doering, Werner J. Dannhauser, and Rada Dyson-Hudson (Cornell Professor of Anthropology)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Maria Epes, George P. Elliott, Barry Edmonston (Cornell Asst. Professor of Sociology), and Welch D. Everman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Leslie A. Fiedler, Robert Folkenflik, and Herbert Finch (also with list of books written by persons affiliated with Cornell University)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Bill Gass, William E. Gibson, Henry Gurlac (Cornell Society for the Humanities), Sandra Gilbert, Christina Groth, and Rose K. Goldsen (Cornell Professor of Sociology)

scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Victor Nee, Mrs. A Robert Noll (Marie Noll), Thomas Nagel, and Linda J. Nessel

scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Pierre Oberling, R.D. O'Brien (Cornell Director of the Ag. School), and Trudi Osborne

scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Thomas Trebitsch Parker, Stuart Peterfreund, Bruce Piasecki, Nancy Potter, William Provine (Cornell Professor of History), and letters to Thomas Pynchon

scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Mark Sagoff, Carl Sagan (Cornell Center for Radiophysics and Space Research), Robert Scott (Cornell Ass. Dean, College of Arts and Sciences), Charles Simic, William Stafford, William J. Smith, Alan Seznec (Cornell Dean College of Arts and Sciences), Michael Swirksky, and G. William Skinner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Eudora Welty, Diane Wakoski, L.P. Williams (Cornell Dept. of History), John H. Whitlock, and Victor F> Weisskopf

scopecontent.p: Letter from Feenie Ziner, Dept. of English, the University of Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4"x6" index cards with addresses and information on Cornell Review contributors and friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Last year of illness. Includes letters from Ep Fogel, John Keats, Carol Mark, Charles E. Smith, George Sheldon, Bob Morgan, Ruth McClane, Archie Ammons, Cipriana Scelba, David McAleavey, Dale R. Corson, Stuart Peterfreund, Anthony B. Turney, Minkoff, Carol Giddings, Deur, Reeve Parker, Dan Schwarz, Daisy Friedman, and David Ray

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remarks, notes, and outline for the English course as well as information for instructors. Also includes sample of similar course taught at University of Wisconsin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from English professors in other institutions; information on poetry lectures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reviews of Writers for Tomorrow; Stories from Epoch: The First Fifty Issues; Dramatic Essays of the Neoclassic Age. Also article by Peter Klappert, "Let Them Eat Wonderbread."

scopecontent.p: Ron Abell, Diane Ackerman, Duane Ackerson, Barry Adams, Hazard Adams, Leigh Agniel, A. Owen Aldridge, Henry Allen, A.R. Ammons, and Patricia Anderson. Also includes newspaper clippings, poetry submissions, and resumes.

Brock, Alan Brown, Walter Brown, Stuart Brown, Joe Bruchac, Jerald Bullis, David Burak, Charles Burkhart, the Burnetts, Eric Burns, and David Burns. Also includes newspaper clippings, poetry submissions, and resumes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound notebook containing poetry written by Baxter Hathaway as a student at University of Michigan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two spiral bound notebooks and one notebook containing notes for possible novels, poetry, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound notebook containing poetry written by Baxter Hathaway for the Hopwood contest.

did.unittitle: Green Fruit: A Book of Poetry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book containing poetry written by Baxter Hathaway while in Michigan.

scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Not certain if written by Baxter Hathaway or one of his students.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various pieces written by Baxter Hathaway. "Rain--Miller Creek"; "The Failure of the Writers"; "Brothers and Sisters"; "Bitterroot Sand"; "Linear Western Town"; "Small Houses"; "Press Club"; "Finches"; "Bear--Cow"; "Rock Creek"; "Gopher Hunting"; "Airport"; "Society"; "Pioneers"; "Ski Cars in May"; "Apple-blossom Time"; "Waterworks Hill"; "Indian in Polson"; "Montana and Iowa"; "Upper Fish Creek"; "Fire on Jumbo"; "Huckleberrying"; "Threat of Fire"; "Mining Woman"; "Mountain Valley"; "Arlee Tavern"; "Flora"; "West as Colony"; "Ghostly at Night: Winter".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short essays written while at Cornell. "The Edge of Fear"; "The Lucretian 'Return upon Ourselves' in Tragic Theory"; "Regionalism and the Atomic Bomb"; "Where are the Snows of Yesteryear? In Memory of Joseph Alexander Altsheler"; "Eagle Forgotten".

did.unittitle: "The Poet's Art and the Poet's Furor"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft of piece by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recollections of Baxter's maternal grandmother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings about the literary magazine including one titled, "Epoch: Or How to Win By Losing." Also list of first year editorial staff, response to request for piece on collegiate writing programs, and essay on the state of fiction and poetry in the contemporary world.

did.unittitle: Essays on the Writing Activities at Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cornell Writing Program and Those University Writers' Workshops

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publications from the 14th Festival of Contemporary Arts in which Baxter Hathaway's poetry is featured

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncertain as to what folder contains. [Appears to be a journal written by Hathaway from July to November of unspecified year.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Essay "The Romantic Criticism of Shakespeare and the Early Nineteenth Century Drama"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Relation Between Modern Verse Forms and the Verse Forms Used by Robert Browning." Written by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reading Notes of Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Theories of the Function of Tragedy in French Criticism at the Time of the Formation of the Neo-Classic Doctrines." Written by Baxter Hathaway at the University of Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publications in which Baxter Hathaway was featured

did.unittitle: Gotthold Ephraim Lessing and His Theories of the Effect of Tragedy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper written by Baxter Hathaway while at the University of Michigan

did.unittitle: The Waves of the Ocean: An Unserious Play in Five Acts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript written by Baxter Hathaway and entered under "A. J. Jochim" for the drama division of the Hopwood Contest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript written by Baxter Hathaway and entered under "A. J. Jochim" for the essay division of the Hopwood Contest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript written by Baxter Hathaway and entered under "A. J. Jochim" for the fiction division of the Hopwood Contest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Essays written by Baxter Hathaway when he was a student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter Hathaway when he was a student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Essays and papers written by Baxter Hathaway when he was a student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mementos related to Baxter Hathaway's college experience. Includes Kaledograph and publication for the Avery Hopwood and Jule Hopwood Awards in Creative Writing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items related to Hathaway's career as college professor. Includes letters announcing promotions and raises.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A sample of advisor administrative activity on Cornell's campus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record card for English major and Hathaway's advisee, Ronald Sukenick

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A sample of Hathaway's record keeping

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to the internal activities of the Cornell English Department

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Student reports saved by Hathaway. Includes newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional correspondence. Includes letter from E.B. White granting permission to reprint "Once More to the Lake"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Surveys sent by Hathaway to the curriculum committee regarding the educational purposes of an English department. Includes a response to this question from W. D. Snodgrass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters and bills of the literary magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Baxter's son, James Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by David McAleavey and sent to Hathaway for review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Wahl as a student at Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Warren as a student at Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry written by Hathaway's students. Includes submissions from Richard Farina, Joanna Russ, Dorothy Gilbert, Ronald Sukenick, Cliff Irving, Steve Tapscott, and Sandra Mortola. Also include newspaper clippings and copies of the Penny Sheet..

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to be a plan for a poetry anthology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to be a plan for a poetry anthology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items related to Cornell University

did.unittitle: The English Sentence: Materials for a Study of Problems of Writing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Textbook written by Baxter Hathaway while Assistant Professor of English at Montana State University

did.unittitle: The English Sentence: Materials for a Study of Problems of Writing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprint of above mentioned textbook written by Baxter Hathaway while Assistant Professor of English at Cornell University.

did.unittitle: Algernon Charles Swinburne: His Ideas and His Practice Concerning His Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thesis written by Baxter Hathaway while at University of Michigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound typescript by Baxter Hathaway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound typescript submitted by A. J. Joachim for the Hopwood Contest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from John Atkins, W. H. Auden, James Ballard, Warren Beck, Betty Bandel, Bruce Berlind, Marjorie Boulton, Steven Berg, Ray Bradbury, Linda Brown, Neil Brennan, Herschel Brickell, Anthony Bridwell, jean Burden, Beverly Brunson, Cid Corman, Judson Crews, F. E. Caldwell, Sarah Dona, LeGarde Doughty, Charles Eaton, Stanley Elkin, George Elliott, Ronald Ellis, John Ferrone, Leslie Fredler, Anne Fremantle, Wingate Froscher, and Northrop Frye.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Emillie Glen, E. S. Green, E. V. Griffith, William Grounge, Edwin Gross, Ralph Gustafson, James Hall, Albert Herzing, Clellon Holmes, Dick Horberg, Barbara Howes, Louis Johnson, August Kadow, Sylvan Karchmer, Bertha Klausner, Julius Laffal, Arline Levinson, Ethel Lewis, Robert Lewis, Louis Locke, Robert Lowry, Roy Marz, James May, nolan Miller, Stephen Minot, and Merrill Moore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from William Owens, Jean Paradise, Henry Popkin, Henry Rago, Marie Rexroth, Robert Riskin, Abraham Rothberg, Miriam Rugel, Joseph Stern, Ralph Samuelson, Marion Saunders, W. T. Scott, Margaret Shedd, Jo Sinclair, and David Stacton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Constance Urdang, Peter Viereck, Vore Villa, Geoffrey Wagner, Jan Wahl, John Warner, Henrietta Weigel, Donald Wesely, miles White, David Wilkins, Henry Wilson, Harold Witt, and Donald J. Young.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from other "little magazines" expressing congratulations for Epoch's success.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Addresses of contributors and subscribers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: David Warren.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peter Klappert and "J.K.M."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Items related to university life--Cornell Alumni News featuring Hathaway and the writing program. Also a letter to President Perkins from the faculty proposing the establishment of an organization for the creative arts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Mountaineer literary magazine. Forward by Baxter Hathaway (Fall 1942); "Letter to my students" (Fall 1943).

scopecontent.p: Mock-crocodile wallet containing postcards of the T.S. Bremen, Flagship of North German Lloyd

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry by David Sykes, Rob Patton, A. R. Ammons, Stuart Peterfreund, William Hathaway, and William Matthews

did.unittitle: Celebration of the Arts at Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of signed poetry--Marie Matthews, A. R. Ammons, Bruce Kawin, William Matthews, John Gill, Jerald Bullis, David Sykes, Arline Levinson, Clark Kimball, and Daniel Berrigan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posters of poetry readings on the Cornell campus. Poets featured include: Galaway Kinnell, Donald Hall, Robert Duncan, Ralph Salisbury, R. V. Cassill, Jim Bertolino and Lynn Shoemaker, John Logan, Steven Katz, Mark Teicher, Bill Matthews and Clara Bulkely, Josephine Jacobsen, John Gill, Steve Tapscott, Anselm Parlatore, Ann Pitrone, Allen Laurance and Foresta Wieder, Stephen Shrader, Peter Klappert, A. R. Ammons, Richard Howard, Ellen Tifft and Douglass Eichhorn, Jean West MacKenzie and Geof Hewitt, Daniel Cassidy, Mother Mallard, Philip Jones and Ronald Sukenick, Marge Piercy, Adam Berger and Anne Pitrone, Diane Wakoski, Paul Hannigan, Adrienne Rich, Michael Benedikt, Robert Allen, Rob Patton, David Ray, Ai, Michael Brownstein, Ishmael Reed, John Logan, Norman Jordan, and David Gitin.
